title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.imageAlt: Close-up of perfectly charred and juicy Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken skewers with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich, smoky flavor of authentic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, made easy at home.

description: Experience the authentic taste of India with our Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, featuring succulent chicken marinated in a rich, spicy yogurt blend. Cooked over charcoal, it develops an irresistible smoky flavor and tender texture that is truly a culinary delight. This recipe brings the magic of a traditional tandoor oven right to your backyard grill.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken for First Marinade
instructions.text: Thoroughly wash and pat dry the chicken pieces. Make 2-3 deep slits on each piece to allow the marinade to penetrate deeply. In a large bowl, combine the chicken with ginger-garlic paste, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, and turmeric powder. Mix well, ensuring the chicken is fully coated. Set aside for 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate large bowl, whisk together the thick plain yogurt, remaining 1 tablespoon lemon juice, remaining 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, tandoori masala, crushed kasuri methi, remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt, mustard oil, and red food color (if using). Mix until a smooth, vibrant marinade forms.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces from the first marinade into the second yogurt-spice marinade. Ensure each piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor and tenderness. The longer it marinates, the better the taste.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a medium-high heat zone. The coals should be glowing red with a light layer of ash. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the oiled hot grill grates. Grill for about 25-35 minutes, turning every 5-7 minutes to ensure even cooking and charring. Baste the chicken occasionally with any leftover marinade or a little oil during the last 10 minutes of cooking to keep it moist and enhance flavor.

instructions.title: Check Doneness and Rest
instructions.text: The chicken is cooked through when its internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) and the juices run clear. The exterior should have a beautiful charred crust. Remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the grilled Tandoori Chicken to a serving platter. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro and serve immediately with lemon wedges, mint chutney, and sliced onions for a complete Indian dinner.

tips: For maximum flavor and tenderness, marinate the chicken for at least 12-24 hours. The longer the marination, the more deeply the flavors will penetrate the meat.

To achieve an extra smoky flavor without a traditional tandoor, you can place a small bowl of hot, smoking charcoal or a smoking wood chip packet directly on the grill during the last few minutes of cooking, carefully covering the grill.
Always pat the chicken pieces very dry before applying the first marinade. This helps the spices adhere better to the chicken and allows for a more effective charring and browning on the grill.
article:
Unleash the tantalizing aroma of a classic Indian feast right in your backyard! There’s something undeniably magical about Tandoori Chicken – its vibrant color, succulent texture, and that signature smoky flavor. While traditionally cooked in a clay tandoor oven, we’re going to show you how to achieve that authentic taste and tenderness with a charcoal grill, making it an accessible and incredibly rewarding Indian dinner recipe for any home cook. Get ready to transform your ordinary weeknight into an extraordinary culinary adventure with the irresistible **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken**.

## What is Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken?

Tandoori Chicken is a world-renowned Indian dish originating from the Punjab region. It features chicken marinated in yogurt and a blend of aromatic spices, then traditionally cooked at high heat in a tandoor, a cylindrical clay oven. The genius of using a charcoal grill to recreate this experience lies in its ability to mimic the tandoor's intense, even heat and, crucially, impart that characteristic smoky flavor that’s simply unmatched by gas or electric ovens. The yogurt in the marinade acts as a tenderizer, while the spices infuse the chicken with layers of complex flavor. When cooked over charcoal, the chicken develops a beautiful char on the outside, locking in juices and creating a delightful contrast of textures. This method ensures your **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken** is not just cooked, but truly transformed into a smoky, spicy, and incredibly tender masterpiece. It's a quintessential Indian dinner recipe that promises a burst of flavor in every bite.

## Ingredients You Need for Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

Creating the perfect Tandoori Chicken starts with high-quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need:

**For the Chicken:**
*   **Chicken:** 2.5-3 lbs (1.1-1.4 kg) chicken pieces – drumsticks, thighs, or a whole chicken cut into quarters. Bone-in, skin-on pieces are preferred for maximum flavor and moisture, but boneless can also work.

**For the Marinade:**
*   **Plain Yogurt:** 1 cup (240ml) full-fat plain yogurt. Greek yogurt, strained to remove excess water, works exceptionally well for a thicker marinade.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 2 tablespoons, freshly ground or store-bought.
*   **Lemon Juice:** 2 tablespoons, freshly squeezed.
*   **Kashmiri Red Chili Powder:** 2 tablespoons. This provides a beautiful red color and mild heat. Adjust to your spice preference.
*   **Turmeric Powder:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Cumin Powder:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Coriander Powder:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Garam Masala:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Salt:** 1.5 teaspoons, or to taste.
*   **Optional (for traditional color):** A few drops of red food coloring.
*   **Optional (for extra flavor):** 1 teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed.

**For Grilling:**
*   **Oil:** 2 tablespoons vegetable or canola oil, for basting.
*   **Skewers:** Metal skewers are ideal for even cooking and easy handling.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

Follow these steps to create an authentic and delicious **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken** in your backyard:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Rinse the chicken pieces and pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make 2-3 deep slits into each piece of chicken. These scores allow the marinade to penetrate deeply, ensuring maximum flavor and tenderness.
2.  **Prepare the Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, salt, and any optional ingredients like red food coloring or kasoori methi. Mix everything thoroughly until you have a smooth, well-combined paste.
3.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the scored chicken pieces to the marinade, ensuring each piece is completely coated. Use your hands to really work the marinade into the slits. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or ideally overnight (up to 24 hours) for the best flavor and tenderization.
4.  **Prepare Your Charcoal Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the coals for two-zone cooking: a mound of coals on one side for direct, high heat, and an empty space on the other for indirect, lower heat. The coals are ready when they are mostly ash-white and glowing. Clean your grill grates and lightly oil them.
5.  **Skewer and Grill:** Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto metal skewers. Do not overcrowd them, leaving a little space between each piece to ensure even cooking. Place the skewers over the direct heat initially for 5-7 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until the chicken has a nice char and develops that characteristic Tandoori color.
6.  **Cook Through:** Once charred, move the skewers to the indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and continue cooking, turning every 5-7 minutes, for another 20-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through. Baste the chicken with oil or any leftover cooked-off marinade every 10 minutes to keep it moist and flavorful.
7.  **Check for Doneness:** The chicken is done when an internal meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part (without touching the bone) reads 165°F (74°C), and the juices run clear.
8.  **Rest and Serve:** Remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes before serving. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in incredibly tender and flavorful chicken.

## Pro Tips for the Best Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

*   **Deep Scoring is Key:** Don't be shy with those slits! They are vital for the marinade to penetrate and work its magic.
*   **Marinate Longer:** While 4-6 hours is good, an overnight marinade (up to 24 hours) yields significantly more flavorful and tender chicken. The longer the chicken soaks in that spicy, tangy goodness, the better.
*   **Use Full-Fat Yogurt:** Full-fat yogurt not only adds richness but also helps the marinade adhere better to the chicken. If using regular yogurt, strain it through cheesecloth for an hour to remove excess water, creating a thicker base.
*   **Master Grill Zones:** The initial direct heat creates the beautiful char and crust, while the indirect heat allows the chicken to cook through gently without burning. This two-zone cooking is crucial for perfect results.
*   **Don't Overcook:** Tandoori Chicken is best when juicy. Use a meat thermometer to ensure it reaches 165°F (74°C) and then remove it promptly.
*   **Baste Regularly:** Brushing the chicken with oil or leftover cooked marinade during the indirect cooking phase helps keep it moist and enhances the flavor.
*   **Preheat Your Skewers:** If using metal skewers, preheating them slightly can help prevent the chicken from sticking.

## Serving Suggestions for Tandoori Chicken

This **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken** is a feast for the senses on its own, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments. Here are some serving suggestions to complete your Indian dinner recipe:

*   **Breads:** Warm naan bread, roti, or paratha are perfect for scooping up the chicken and any residual juices.
*   **Chutneys:** A cooling mint chutney or a tangy tamarind chutney provides a wonderful contrast to the smoky chicken.
*   **Raita:** A simple yogurt raita (yogurt mixed with grated cucumber, cumin, and mint) is a fantastic palate cleanser.
*   **Salad:** A simple onion and cucumber salad, dressed with lemon juice and a sprinkle of chaat masala, adds freshness.
*   **Rice:** Basmati rice or jeera rice (cumin rice) makes for a wholesome and satisfying side.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before eating brightens all the flavors.

## Variations and Substitutions for Tandoori Chicken

While the classic **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken** is a winner, there are many ways to adapt this Indian dinner recipe to your taste and dietary needs:

*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Kashmiri red chili powder (or add some cayenne for extra heat) to suit your preference.
*   **Color:** If you prefer a more natural look, simply omit the red food coloring. The turmeric and chili powder will still give it a lovely golden-orange hue.
*   **Vegetarian Tandoori:** The same marinade works beautifully with paneer (Indian cheese), mushrooms, cauliflower florets, or even firm tofu. Simply cut them into large pieces and marinate for 1-2 hours before grilling.
*   **Other Proteins:** Try this marinade with lamb chops, fish fillets (reduce marinating time to 30 minutes), or even large prawns for a different take on tandoori.
*   **Herbaceous Twist:** Add chopped fresh cilantro or mint to the marinade for an extra layer of freshness.
*   **No Charcoal Grill? No Problem (Almost!):** While charcoal provides the best flavor, you can still make Tandoori Chicken using a gas grill (follow the two-zone cooking method) or even your oven broiler. For oven cooking, preheat your broiler, place chicken on a rack over a foil-lined baking sheet, and broil for 8-10 minutes per side, turning until cooked through and nicely charred.

Mastering the art of **Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken** at home is a rewarding experience that brings the vibrant flavors of India right to your table. With its smoky char, tender meat, and aromatic spices, it’s truly an Indian dinner recipe that will impress and delight. So fire up that grill, gather your ingredients, and prepare to embark on a delicious culinary journey!
